response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled software craftsman with a broad background in distributed systems, event-driven architectures, and full-stack web development, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. His C++ experience is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project, while his career has been overwhelmingly TypeScript/JavaScript/DLang-focused. The role demands deep, production-grade C++ expertise as its core competency. Additionally, there is no evidence of IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle knowledge, industrial protocol familiarity (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), or industrial automation domain experience beyond peripheral exposure at Altom with robotic arms.
strengths: Solid event-driven and distributed systems background (GISCollective, Dunnhumby micro-services handling millions of requests/second)
Node.js and React experience satisfies the advantageous skills listed in the JD
Strong software craftsmanship practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and code quality mentoring
concerns: C++ proficiency is superficial — roughly six months at NI and a thesis project over a 15+ year career dominated by TypeScript, DLang, and JavaScript
No demonstrated knowledge of IEC 62443, SDL practices, or industrial cybersecurity concepts required for the role
No experience with industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850) or the automation/OT domain the employer operates in
